page: Amazon Web Services m6a.48xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the m6a.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 286,813.92 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 114,978.35 in PassMark CPU evaluations. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 12,131.63 GB/sec, alongside strong uncached large-block read and write speeds. Cryptographic and mathematical capabilities are highly rated, as evidenced by top-tier PassMark encryption and physics scores. Database and caching performance are also notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 175.75 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for large-scale deployments that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Because local storage is absent, all disk I/O must go through network-attached storage, though the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps to mitigate potential bottlenecks. The server is well-suited for highly multi-threaded applications, enterprise database hosting, high-throughput caching layers, software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, the instance relies on its 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage connectivity. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput Redis caching capabilities. It is designed for resource-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as enterprise database operations, software compilation, and high-volume static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.48xlarge is a general-purpose server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.48xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, massive DDR4 memory, and high-speed baseline networking.
bullet_points: Powered by a 192-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory at a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, databases, and high-throughput caching
